15	import warnings, importlib, sys
16	from packaging.version import Version
17	import os, re, subprocess, inspect, functools
18	import numpy as np
19	
20	# Fix some issues before importing other packages
21	from .import_fixes import fix_message_factory_issue
22	fix_message_factory_issue(); del fix_message_factory_issue;
23	
24	# Check if modules that need patching are already imported
25	critical_modules = ['trl', 'transformers', 'peft']
26	already_imported = [mod for mod in critical_modules if mod in sys.modules]
27	
28	# This check is critical because Unsloth optimizes these libraries by modifying
29	# their code at import time. If they're imported first, the original (slower,
30	# more memory-intensive) implementations will be used instead of Unsloth's
31	# optimized versions, potentially causing OOM errors or slower training.
32	
33	if already_imported:
34	    # stacklevel=2 makes warning point to user's import line rather than this library code,
35	    # showing them exactly where to fix the import order in their script
36	    warnings.warn(
37	        f"WARNING: Unsloth should be imported before {', '.join(already_imported)} "
38	        f"to ensure all optimizations are applied. Your code may run slower or encounter "
39	        f"memory issues without these optimizations.\n\n"
40	        f"Please restructure your imports with 'import unsloth' at the top of your file.",
41	        stacklevel = 2,
42	    )
43	pass
44	
45	# Unsloth currently does not work on multi GPU setups - sadly we are a 2 brother team so
46	# enabling it will require much more work, so we have to prioritize. Please understand!
47	# We do have a beta version, which you can contact us about!
48	# Thank you for your understanding and we appreciate it immensely!
49	
50	# Fixes https://github.com/unslothai/unsloth/issues/1266
51	os.environ["PROTOCOL_BUFFERS_PYTHON_IMPLEMENTATION"] = "python"
52	
53	# [TODO] Check why some GPUs don't work
54	#    "pinned_use_cuda_host_register:True,"\
55	#    "pinned_num_register_threads:8"
56	
57	# Log Unsloth is being used
58	os.environ["UNSLOTH_IS_PRESENT"] = "1"
59	
60	# Try importing PyTorch and check version
61	try:
62	    import torch
63	except ModuleNotFoundError:
64	    raise ImportError(
65	        "Unsloth: Pytorch is not installed. Go to https://pytorch.org/.\n"\
66	        "We have some installation instructions on our Github page."
67	    )
68	except Exception as exception:
69	    raise exception
70	pass
71	
72	@functools.cache
73	def is_hip():
74	    return bool(getattr(getattr(torch, "version", None), "hip", None))
75	pass
76	
77	@functools.cache
78	def get_device_type():
79	    if hasattr(torch, "cuda") and torch.cuda.is_available():
80	        if is_hip():
81	            return "hip"
82	        return "cuda"
83	    elif hasattr(torch, "xpu") and torch.xpu.is_available():
84	        return "xpu"
85	    raise NotImplementedError("Unsloth currently only works on NVIDIA GPUs and Intel GPUs.")
86	pass
87	DEVICE_TYPE : str = get_device_type()
88	
89	@functools.cache
90	def get_device_count():
91	    if DEVICE_TYPE in ("cuda", "hip"):
92	        return torch.cuda.device_count()
93	    elif DEVICE_TYPE == "xpu":
94	        return torch.xpu.device_count()
95	    else:
96	        return 1
97	pass
98	
99	DEVICE_COUNT : int = get_device_count()
100	
101	# Reduce VRAM usage by reducing fragmentation
102	# And optimize pinning of memory
103	# TODO(billishyahao): need to add hip related optimization...
104	if (DEVICE_TYPE in ("cuda", "hip")) and (os.environ.get("UNSLOTH_VLLM_STANDBY", "0")=="0"):
105	    os.environ["PYTORCH_CUDA_ALLOC_CONF"] = \
106	        "expandable_segments:True,"\
107	        "roundup_power2_divisions:[32:256,64:128,256:64,>:32]"
108	elif (DEVICE_TYPE in ("cuda", "hip")) and (os.environ.get("UNSLOTH_VLLM_STANDBY", "0")=="1") and \
109	    ("expandable_segments:True" in os.environ.get("PYTORCH_CUDA_ALLOC_CONF", "")):
110	    warnings.warn(
111	        "Unsloth: `UNSLOTH_VLLM_STANDBY` is on, but requires `expandable_segments` to be off.\n"\
112	        "We will remove `expandable_segments`.",
113	        stacklevel = 2,
114	    )
115	    os.environ["PYTORCH_CUDA_ALLOC_CONF"] = re.sub(
116	        r"expandable\_segments\:True\,?",
117	        "",
118	        os.environ["PYTORCH_CUDA_ALLOC_CONF"],
119	    )
120	pass
121	# We support Pytorch 2
122	# Fixes https://github.com/unslothai/unsloth/issues/38
123	torch_version = str(re.match(r"[0-9\.]{3,}", str(torch.__version__)).group(0)).split(".")
124	major_torch, minor_torch = torch_version[0], torch_version[1]
125	major_torch, minor_torch = int(major_torch), int(minor_torch)
126	if (major_torch < 2):
127	    raise ImportError("Unsloth only supports Pytorch 2 for now. Please update your Pytorch to 2.1.\n"\
128	                      "We have some installation instructions on our Github page.")
129	elif (major_torch == 2) and (minor_torch < 2):
130	    # Disable expandable_segments
131	    del os.environ["PYTORCH_CUDA_ALLOC_CONF"]
132	pass
133	
134	# CCE fails on Torch 2.8 and above
135	# OutOfResources: out of resource: shared memory, Required: 98304, Hardware limit: 65536. Reducing block sizes or `num_stages`
136	if (major_torch >= 2 and minor_torch >= 8) or (major_torch > 2):
137	    os.environ["UNSLOTH_ENABLE_CCE"] = "0"
138	pass
139	
140	# Fix other issues
141	import importlib.util
142	from pathlib import Path
143	from importlib.metadata import version as importlib_version
144	from .import_fixes import fix_xformers_performance_issue
145	fix_xformers_performance_issue(); del fix_xformers_performance_issue;
146	from .import_fixes import fix_vllm_aimv2_issue
147	fix_vllm_aimv2_issue(); del fix_vllm_aimv2_issue;
148	from .import_fixes import ignore_logger_messages
149	ignore_logger_messages(); del ignore_logger_messages;
150	
151	# Torch 2.4 has including_emulation
152	if DEVICE_TYPE == "cuda":
153	    major_version, minor_version = torch.cuda.get_device_capability()
154	    SUPPORTS_BFLOAT16 = (major_version >= 8)
155	
156	    old_is_bf16_supported = torch.cuda.is_bf16_supported
157	    if "including_emulation" in str(inspect.signature(old_is_bf16_supported)):
158	        def is_bf16_supported(including_emulation = False):
159	            return old_is_bf16_supported(including_emulation)
160	        torch.cuda.is_bf16_supported = is_bf16_supported
161	    else:
162	        def is_bf16_supported(): return SUPPORTS_BFLOAT16
163	        torch.cuda.is_bf16_supported = is_bf16_supported
164	    pass
165	elif DEVICE_TYPE == "hip":
166	    SUPPORTS_BFLOAT16 = torch.cuda.is_bf16_supported()
167	elif DEVICE_TYPE == "xpu":
168	    # torch.xpu.is_bf16_supported() does not have including_emulation
169	    # set SUPPORTS_BFLOAT16 as torch.xpu.is_bf16_supported()
170	    SUPPORTS_BFLOAT16 = torch.xpu.is_bf16_supported()
171	pass
172	
173	# For Gradio HF Spaces?
174	# if "SPACE_AUTHOR_NAME" not in os.environ and "SPACE_REPO_NAME" not in os.environ:
175	import triton
176	if DEVICE_TYPE == "cuda":
177	    libcuda_dirs = lambda: None
178	    if Version(triton.__version__) >= Version("3.0.0"):
179	        try: from triton.backends.nvidia.driver import libcuda_dirs
180	        except: pass
181	    else: from triton.common.build import libcuda_dirs
182	
183	    # Try loading bitsandbytes and triton
184	    import bitsandbytes as bnb
185	    try:
186	        cdequantize_blockwise_fp32 = bnb.functional.lib.cdequantize_blockwise_fp32
187	        libcuda_dirs()
188	    except:
189	        warnings.warn(
190	            "Unsloth: Running `ldconfig /usr/lib64-nvidia` to link CUDA."\
191	        )
192	
193	        if os.path.exists("/usr/lib64-nvidia"):
194	            os.system("ldconfig /usr/lib64-nvidia")
195	        elif os.path.exists("/usr/local"):
196	            # Sometimes bitsandbytes cannot be linked properly in Runpod for example
197	            possible_cudas = subprocess.check_output(["ls", "-al", "/usr/local"]).decode("utf-8").split("\n")
198	            find_cuda = re.compile(r"[\s](cuda\-[\d\.]{2,})$")
199	            possible_cudas = [find_cuda.search(x) for x in possible_cudas]
200	            possible_cudas = [x.group(1) for x in possible_cudas if x is not None]
201	
202	            # Try linking cuda folder, or everything in local
203	            if len(possible_cudas) == 0:
204	                os.system("ldconfig /usr/local/")
205	            else:
206	                find_number = re.compile(r"([\d\.]{2,})")
207	                latest_cuda = np.argsort([float(find_number.search(x).group(1)) for x in possible_cudas])[::-1][0]
208	                latest_cuda = possible_cudas[latest_cuda]
209	                os.system(f"ldconfig /usr/local/{latest_cuda}")
210	        pass
211	
212	        importlib.reload(bnb)
213	        importlib.reload(triton)
214	        try:
215	            libcuda_dirs = lambda: None
216	            if Version(triton.__version__) >= Version("3.0.0"):
217	                try: from triton.backends.nvidia.driver import libcuda_dirs
218	                except: pass
219	            else: from triton.common.build import libcuda_dirs
220	            cdequantize_blockwise_fp32 = bnb.functional.lib.cdequantize_blockwise_fp32
221	            libcuda_dirs()
222	        except:
223	            warnings.warn(
224	                "Unsloth: CUDA is not linked properly.\n"\
225	                "Try running `python -m bitsandbytes` then `python -m xformers.info`\n"\
226	                "We tried running `ldconfig /usr/lib64-nvidia` ourselves, but it didn't work.\n"\
227	                "You need to run in your terminal `sudo ldconfig /usr/lib64-nvidia` yourself, then import Unsloth.\n"\
228	                "Also try `sudo ldconfig /usr/local/cuda-xx.x` - find the latest cuda version.\n"\
229	                "Unsloth will still run for now, but maybe it might crash - let's hope it works!"
230	            )
231	    pass
232	elif DEVICE_TYPE == "hip":
233	    # NO-OP for rocm device
234	    pass
235	elif DEVICE_TYPE == "xpu":
236	    # currently intel xpu will not support bnb, will add support in the future
237	    # TODO: check triton for intel installed properly.
238	    pass
239	
240	# Check for unsloth_zoo
241	try:
242	    unsloth_zoo_version = importlib_version("unsloth_zoo")
243	    if Version(unsloth_zoo_version) < Version("2025.9.6"):

137	def _free_cached_model(model):
138	    from huggingface_hub import scan_cache_dir
139	    cached_repos = list(scan_cache_dir().repos)
140	
141	    # Go through every cached repo, and delete the one that matches the model we want to save.
142	    # Can save 4GB of disk space - useful for Kaggle systems.
143	    for cached_repo in cached_repos:

159	def _merge_lora(layer, name):
160	
161	    bias = getattr(layer, "bias", None)
162	    if isinstance(layer, (Bnb_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear)):
163	        # Is LoRA so we need to merge!
164	        W, quant_state, A, B, s, bias = get_lora_parameters_bias(layer)
165	        if quant_state is not None:
166	            dtype = quant_state.dtype if type(quant_state) is not list else quant_state[2]
167	            W = fast_dequantize(W, quant_state)
168	        else:
169	            dtype = W.dtype
170	        W = W.to(torch.float32).t()
171	        # W = W.t()
172	
173	        if A is not None:
174	            # sAB = (A.t().to(torch.float32) @ (s * B.t().to(torch.float32)))
175	            # W += sAB
176	            W.addmm_(A.t().to(torch.float32), B.t().to(torch.float32), alpha = s)
177	            # W.addmm_(A.t().to(W.dtype), B.t().to(W.dtype), alpha = s)
178	            # if not torch.isfinite(W).all():
179	            maximum_element = torch.max(W.min().abs(), W.max())
180	            if not torch.isfinite(maximum_element).item():
181	                raise ValueError(f"Unsloth: Merge failed.\n{name} has some elements = infinity.")
182	        pass
183	        W = W.t().to(dtype)
184	    else:

28	class LoRA_MLP(torch.autograd.Function):
29	    """
30	    ### LoRA weights
31	    G = G + Ag @ Bg
32	    U = U + Au @ Bu
33	    W = W + Aw @ Bw
34	
35	    ### SwiGLU(X)
36	    e = X @ G
37	    f = e * sigmoid(e)
38	    g = X @ U
39	    h = f * g
40	    i = h @ W
41	
42	    ### Backpropagation chain rule
43	    See our blog post for more details
44	
45	    df = sigmoid(e) * (1 - f) + f
46	    dC/dW = h.T @ dY
47	    dC/dU = X.T @ (D @ W.T * f)
48	    dC/dG = X.T @ (D @ W.T * df * g)
49	
50	    ### Down projection LoRA weights
51	    dC/dAw = dC/dW @ B.T
52	    dC/dBw = A.T @ dC/dW
53	    dC/dAw =       h.T @ dY @ B.T
54	    dC/dBw = A.T @ h.T @ dY
55	
56	    ### Up projection LoRA weights
57	    dC/dAu =       X.T @ (D @ W.T * f) @ B.T
58	    dC/dBu = A.T @ X.T @ (D @ W.T * f)
59	
60	    ### Gate projection LoRA weights
61	    dC/dAg =       X.T @ (D @ W.T * df * g) @ B.T
62	    dC/dBg = A.T @ X.T @ (D @ W.T * df * g)
63	
64	    Don't forget to see our blog post for more details!
65	    """
66	    @staticmethod
67	    @torch_amp_custom_fwd
68	    def forward(ctx, X : torch.Tensor,
69	                gateW, gateW_quant, gateA, gateB, gateS,
70	                  upW,   upW_quant, upA,   upB,   upS,
71	                downW, downW_quant, downA, downB, downS,
72	                _forward_function, _backward_function,
73	                inplace = True,):
74	        dtype = X.dtype
75	
76	        e = matmul_lora(X, gateW, gateW_quant, gateA, gateB, gateS)
77	        g = matmul_lora(X,   upW,   upW_quant,   upA,   upB,   upS)
78	        h = _forward_function(e, g)
79	        i = matmul_lora(h, downW, downW_quant, downA, downB, downS)
80	
81	        ctx.custom_saved_tensors = (
82	            gateW, gateW_quant, gateS,
83	            upW, upW_quant, upS,
84	            downW, downW_quant, downS,
85	            _backward_function,
86	        )
87	        ctx.save_for_backward(gateA, gateB, upA, upB, downA, downB,
88	                              X, e, g)
89	        ctx.inplace = inplace
90	        return i
91	    pass
92

97	        gateW, gateW_quant, gateS, upW, upW_quant, upS, downW, downW_quant, downS, \
98	            _backward_function = ctx.custom_saved_tensors

137	def _free_cached_model(model):
138	    from huggingface_hub import scan_cache_dir
139	    cached_repos = list(scan_cache_dir().repos)
140	
141	    # Go through every cached repo, and delete the one that matches the model we want to save.
142	    # Can save 4GB of disk space - useful for Kaggle systems.
143	    for cached_repo in cached_repos:
144	        if cached_repo.repo_id == model.config._name_or_path:
145	            remove_cache_commit = list(cached_repo.revisions)[0].commit_hash
146	            delete_strategy = scan_cache_dir().delete_revisions(remove_cache_commit,)
147	
148	            logger.warning_once(
149	                "Unsloth: Will remove a cached repo with size " + \
150	                delete_strategy.expected_freed_size_str,
151	            )
152	
153	            delete_strategy.execute()
154	        pass
155	    pass

159	def _merge_lora(layer, name):
160	
161	    bias = getattr(layer, "bias", None)
162	    if isinstance(layer, (Bnb_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear)):
163	        # Is LoRA so we need to merge!
164	        W, quant_state, A, B, s, bias = get_lora_parameters_bias(layer)
165	        if quant_state is not None:
166	            dtype = quant_state.dtype if type(quant_state) is not list else quant_state[2]
167	            W = fast_dequantize(W, quant_state)
168	        else:
169	            dtype = W.dtype
170	        W = W.to(torch.float32).t()
171	        # W = W.t()
172	
173	        if A is not None:
174	            # sAB = (A.t().to(torch.float32) @ (s * B.t().to(torch.float32)))
175	            # W += sAB
176	            W.addmm_(A.t().to(torch.float32), B.t().to(torch.float32), alpha = s)
177	            # W.addmm_(A.t().to(W.dtype), B.t().to(W.dtype), alpha = s)
178	            # if not torch.isfinite(W).all():
179	            maximum_element = torch.max(W.min().abs(), W.max())
180	            if not torch.isfinite(maximum_element).item():
181	                raise ValueError(f"Unsloth: Merge failed.\n{name} has some elements = infinity.")
182	        pass
183	        W = W.t().to(dtype)
184	    else:

67	    if BLOCK_SIZE > MAX_FUSED_SIZE:
68	        raise RuntimeError(f"Cannot launch Triton kernel since n = {n} exceeds "\
69	                           f"the maximum CUDA blocksize = {MAX_FUSED_SIZE}.")
70	    num_warps : int = 4
71	    if   BLOCK_SIZE >= 32768: num_warps = 32
72	    elif BLOCK_SIZE >=  8192: num_warps = 16
73	    elif BLOCK_SIZE >=  2048: num_warps = 8
74	    return BLOCK_SIZE, num_warps

190	def get_lora_parameters(proj):
191	    """
192	    Return a 5-tuple of (weight, weight quant_state, lora A, lora B, and lora scale).
193	    If QAT is enabled, additionally fake quantize the base layer and lora weights.
194	    """
195	    # For DPO or disabled adapters
196	    base_layer = getattr(proj, "base_layer", proj) # (proj.base_layer if hasattr(proj, "base_layer") else proj)
197	    W = base_layer.weight
198	
199	    # Optionally apply fake quantization to base layer weights for QAT
200	    weight_fake_quantizer = getattr(base_layer, "weight_fake_quantizer", None)
201	    if weight_fake_quantizer is not None:
202	        W = weight_fake_quantizer(W)
203	
204	    # if not hasattr(proj, "disable_adapters") or proj.disable_adapters or proj.merged:
205	    if getattr(proj, "disable_adapters", True) or proj.merged:
206	        return W, getattr(W, "quant_state", None), None, None, None
207	    pass
208	
209	    adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapters", None)
210	    if adapter is None: adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapter", ("default"))
211	    adapter = adapter[0]
212	
213	    # Optionally apply fake quantization to lora weights for QAT
214	    lora_A_linear = proj.lora_A[adapter]
215	    lora_B_linear = proj.lora_B[adapter]
216	    lora_A_fake_quantizer = getattr(lora_A_linear, "weight_fake_quantizer", None)
217	    lora_B_fake_quantizer = getattr(lora_B_linear, "weight_fake_quantizer", None)
218	    A = lora_A_linear.weight
219	    B = lora_B_linear.weight
220	    if lora_A_fake_quantizer is not None:
221	        A = lora_A_fake_quantizer(A)
222	    if lora_B_fake_quantizer is not None:
223	        B = lora_B_fake_quantizer(B)
224	
225	    return (
226	        W,
227	        getattr(W, "quant_state", None),
228	        A,
229	        B,
230	        proj.scaling[adapter],
231	    )
232	pass
233

238	    W = base_layer.weight
239	
240	    # if not hasattr(proj, "disable_adapters") or proj.disable_adapters or proj.merged:
241	    if getattr(proj, "disable_adapters", True) or proj.merged:
242	        return W, getattr(W, "quant_state", None), None, None, None, base_layer.bias
243	    pass
244	
245	    adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapters", None)
246	    if adapter is None: adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapter", ("default"))
247	    adapter = adapter[0]
248	
249	    return (
250	        W,
251	        getattr(W, "quant_state", None),
252	        proj.lora_A [adapter].weight,
253	        proj.lora_B [adapter].weight,
254	        proj.scaling[adapter],

260	def _maybe_fake_quantize_activations(X: torch.Tensor, proj: torch.nn.Module) -> torch.Tensor:
261	    """
262	    If QAT is enabled, fake quantize the input activations.
263	    Otherwise, just return the input activations as is.
264	    Weights are fake quantized separately in `get_lora_parameters`.
265	    """
266	    base_layer = getattr(proj, "base_layer", proj)
267	    activation_fake_quantizer = getattr(base_layer, "activation_fake_quantizer", None)
268	    if activation_fake_quantizer is not None:
269	        X = activation_fake_quantizer(X)
270	    return X
271	pass

277	    def fast_dequantize(W, quant_state = None, out = None, use_global_buffer = False):
278	        # TODO: After adding XPU BNB support, check this function
279	        if quant_state is None: return W
280	        if type(quant_state) is not list:
281	            # New quant_state as a class
282	            # https://github.com/TimDettmers/bitsandbytes/pull/763/files
283	            absmax     = quant_state.absmax
284	            shape      = quant_state.shape
285	            dtype      = quant_state.dtype
286	            blocksize  = quant_state.blocksize
287	            offset     = quant_state.offset
288	            state2     = quant_state.state2
289	            absmax2    = state2.absmax
290	            code2      = state2.code
291	            blocksize2 = state2.blocksize
292	        else:
293	            # Old quant_state as a list of lists
294	            absmax, shape, dtype, blocksize, compressed_stats, _, _ = quant_state
295	            offset, state2 = compressed_stats
296	            absmax2, code2, blocksize2, _, _, _, _ = state2
297	        pass
298	        global XPU_STREAMS
299	        device = W.device
300	        device_index = device.index

771	    # https://github.com/ggerganov/llama.cpp/issues/7062
772	    # Weirdly GPU conversion for GGUF breaks??

28	class LoRA_MLP(torch.autograd.Function):
29	    """
30	    ### LoRA weights
31	    G = G + Ag @ Bg
32	    U = U + Au @ Bu
33	    W = W + Aw @ Bw
34	
35	    ### SwiGLU(X)
36	    e = X @ G
37	    f = e * sigmoid(e)
38	    g = X @ U
39	    h = f * g
40	    i = h @ W
41	
42	    ### Backpropagation chain rule
43	    See our blog post for more details
44	
45	    df = sigmoid(e) * (1 - f) + f
46	    dC/dW = h.T @ dY
47	    dC/dU = X.T @ (D @ W.T * f)
48	    dC/dG = X.T @ (D @ W.T * df * g)
49	
50	    ### Down projection LoRA weights
51	    dC/dAw = dC/dW @ B.T
52	    dC/dBw = A.T @ dC/dW
53	    dC/dAw =       h.T @ dY @ B.T
54	    dC/dBw = A.T @ h.T @ dY
55	
56	    ### Up projection LoRA weights
57	    dC/dAu =       X.T @ (D @ W.T * f) @ B.T
58	    dC/dBu = A.T @ X.T @ (D @ W.T * f)
59	
60	    ### Gate projection LoRA weights
61	    dC/dAg =       X.T @ (D @ W.T * df * g) @ B.T
62	    dC/dBg = A.T @ X.T @ (D @ W.T * df * g)
63	
64	    Don't forget to see our blog post for more details!
65	    """
66	    @staticmethod
67	    @torch_amp_custom_fwd
68	    def forward(ctx, X : torch.Tensor,
69	                gateW, gateW_quant, gateA, gateB, gateS,
70	                  upW,   upW_quant, upA,   upB,   upS,
71	                downW, downW_quant, downA, downB, downS,
72	                _forward_function, _backward_function,
73	                inplace = True,):
74	        dtype = X.dtype
75	
76	        e = matmul_lora(X, gateW, gateW_quant, gateA, gateB, gateS)
77	        g = matmul_lora(X,   upW,   upW_quant,   upA,   upB,   upS)
78	        h = _forward_function(e, g)
79	        i = matmul_lora(h, downW, downW_quant, downA, downB, downS)
80

224	class LoRA_QKV(torch.autograd.Function):
225	    """
226	    ### LoRA weights
227	    Wq = Wq + Aq @ Bq
228	    Wk = Wk + Ak @ Bk
229	    Wv = Wv + Av @ Bv
230	    Q = X @ Wq = X @ Wq + X @ Aq @ Bq
231	    K = X @ Wk = X @ Wk + X @ Ak @ Bk
232	    V = X @ Wv = X @ Wv + X @ Av @ Bv
233	
234	    ### Backpropagation chain rule
235	    See our blogpost for more details.
236	
237	    dC/dWq = X.T @ D(Wq)
238	    dC/dWk = X.T @ D(Wk)
239	    dC/dWv = X.T @ D(Wv)
240	    We then sum them all find dC/dX
241	
242	    ### Q projection LoRA weights
243	    dC/dAq =       X.T @ D(Wq) @ B.T
244	    dC/dBq = A.T @ X.T @ D(Wq)
245	
246	    ### K projection LoRA weights
247	    dC/dAk =       X.T @ D(Wk) @ B.T
248	    dC/dBk = A.T @ X.T @ D(Wk)
249	
250	    ### V projection LoRA weights
251	    dC/dAv =       X.T @ D(Wv) @ B.T
252	    dC/dBv = A.T @ X.T @ D(Wv)
253	    """
254	    @staticmethod
255	    @torch_amp_custom_fwd
256	    def forward(ctx, X : torch.Tensor,
257	                QW, QW_quant, QA, QB, QS,
258	                KW, KW_quant, KA, KB, KS,
259	                VW, VW_quant, VA, VB, VS,
260	                inplace = True):
261	        dtype = X.dtype
262	
263	        Q = matmul_lora(X, QW, QW_quant, QA, QB, QS)
264	        K = matmul_lora(X, KW, KW_quant, KA, KB, KS)
265	        V = matmul_lora(X, VW, VW_quant, VA, VB, VS)
266	
267	        ctx.custom_saved_tensors = (
268	            QW, QW_quant, QS,
269	            KW, KW_quant, KS,
270	            VW, VW_quant, VS,
271	        )
272	        ctx.save_for_backward(X, QA, QB, KA, KB, VA, VB,)
273	        ctx.inplace = inplace
274	        return Q, K, V
275	    pass

295	            QA.t(), QB.t(), KA.t(), KB.t(), VA.t(), VB.t()

734:def matmul_lora(X, W, W_quant, A, B, s, out = None):

def matmul_lora(X, W, W_quant, A, B, s, out = None):
    dtype = X.dtype
    W = fast_dequantize(W.t(), W_quant, use_global_buffer = True)

    if X.dim() == 3:
        batch, seq_len, d = X.shape
        X = X.view(-1, X.shape[-1])
        reshape = True
    else:
        reshape = False
    pass
    out = torch_matmul(X, W, out = out)
    if W_quant is not None: del W

    if A is not None:
        # LoRA is enabled
        A, B = A.t(), B.t()
        XA = torch_matmul(X, A.to(dtype))
        out.addmm_(XA, B.to(dtype), alpha = s)
        # out += (X @ A.to(dtype)) @ (s * B.to(dtype))
    pass

    return out.view(batch, seq_len, -1) if reshape else out

Perfect! Now I have a comprehensive understanding. Let me create a final summary document with all the findings:

## Summary: How Unsloth Exposes Save and Export Methods

Based on my analysis of the unsloth codebase, here's a comprehensive overview of how save and export methods are exposed to users:

### Public API Files

**Main entry point:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775476413559-8orraq/unsloth/__init__.py`
- Imports from `unsloth.save` via `from .save import *` (line 263)
- Imports from `unsloth.models` via `from .models import *` (line 261)

**Public API definitions:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775476413559-8orraq/unsloth/models/__init__.py`
- Exports `FastLanguageModel`, `FastVisionModel`, `FastTextModel`, `FastModel` from loader

### Core Save Functions

All core save functionality is in: `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775476413559-8orraq/unsloth/save.py`

**__all__ exports (lines 48-54):**
```python
__all__ = [
    "print_quantization_methods",
    "unsloth_save_model",
    "save_to_gguf",
    "patch_saving_functions",
    "create_huggingface_repo",
]
```

### Monkey-Patching Architecture

**1. Main patching function:** `patch_saving_functions(model, vision = False)` (line 2569)

This function dynamically:
- Wraps the original `push_to_hub` method and replaces it with `unsloth_push_to_hub`
- Stores the original as `model.original_push_to_hub`
- Uses `types.MethodType` to bind new methods to the model instance
- Adds custom save/export methods via monkey-patching

**2. Methods added to models via monkey-patching (lines 2657-2676):**

For language models (when `vision=False`):
```python
model.push_to_hub_merged = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_push_to_hub_merged, model)
model.save_pretrained_merged = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged, model)
model.push_to_hub_gguf = types.MethodType(unsloth_push_to_hub_gguf, model)
model.save_pretrained_gguf = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_gguf, model)
model.save_pretrained_torchao = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao, model)
model.push_to_hub_ggml = types.MethodType(unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_push_to_hub, model)
model.save_pretrained_ggml = types.MethodType(unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_save_locally, model)
```

For vision models (when `vision=True`):
```python
model.push_to_hub_merged = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_push_to_hub_merged, model)
model.save_pretrained_merged = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged, model)
model.push_to_hub_gguf = types.MethodType(save_to_gguf_generic, model)
model.save_pretrained_gguf = types.MethodType(save_to_gguf_generic, model)
model.save_pretrained_torchao = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao, model)
```

### Where Patching is Called

1. **Language models:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775476413559-8orraq/unsloth/models/llama.py` (lines 2188, 2204, 2902)
2. **Vision models:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775476413559-8orraq/unsloth/models/vision.py` (lines 702, 703, 918, 964)
3. **Chat templates:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775476413559-8orraq/unsloth/chat_templates.py` (line 2309) - for tokenizer

### Save Method Functions

**Core save function:**
- `unsloth_save_model()` (line 205) - Main workhorse function that handles all save logic

**Merged saving (wrapper methods for backward compatibility):**
- `unsloth_save_pretrained_merged()` (line 1321) - Instance method wrapper
- `unsloth_push_to_hub_merged()` (line 1364) - Instance method wrapper

**Generic merged saving:**
- `unsloth_generic_save()` (line 2356) - Core implementation for merged saves
- `unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged()` (line 2413) - Public instance method
- `unsloth_generic_push_to_hub_merged()` (line 2456) - Public instance method

**GGUF format:**
- `unsloth_save_pretrained_gguf()` (line 1768) - Save to GGUF locally
- `unsloth_push_to_hub_gguf()` (line 1951) - Push GGUF to Hub

**GGML format:**
- `unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_save_locally()` (line 2211) - Save GGML locally
- `unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_push_to_hub()` (line 2143) - Push GGML to Hub

**TorchAO format:**
- `unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao()` (line 2502) - Save with TorchAO quantization

**Push to Hub overrides:**
- `unsloth_push_to_hub()` - Dynamically created via `exec()` (lines 2586-2640) to wrap the original `push_to_hub` method with Unsloth-specific logic (adds "Unsloth" tags, modifies commit messages, etc.)

### Key Classes Where Patching Occurs

1. **FastLanguageModel** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775476413559-8orraq/unsloth/models/loader.py` (line 107)
   - Inherits from `FastLlamaModel`
   - `patch_saving_functions()` called on loaded models

2. **Vision models** - Use `FastBaseModel` with vision-specific patching

3. **Text-to-speech and other models** - Also get patched through their respective loader classes

### Exposure Pattern Summary

**Direct method additions (7 new methods per model):**
- `model.save_pretrained_merged()`
- `model.push_to_hub_merged()`
- `model.save_pretrained_gguf()`
- `model.push_to_hub_gguf()`
- `model.save_pretrained_torchao()`
- `model.push_to_hub_ggml()` / `model.save_pretrained_ggml()`

**Overridden method (wrapped):**
- `model.push_to_hub()` - Enhanced version that stores original as `model.original_push_to_hub()`

**Through public module:**
- `unsloth.unsloth_save_model()` - Core save function exposed publicly
- `unsloth.patch_saving_functions()` - For manual patching if needed

This design allows unsloth to seamlessly extend HuggingFace models with additional export formats while preserving the original methods and maintaining backward compatibility.

## Comprehensive Report: LoRA Adapter Weights Storage and Access in Unsloth

Based on my thorough analysis of the unsloth codebase, here's a detailed breakdown of how LoRA adapter weights (A and B matrices) are stored and accessed:

---

### **1. LoRA Weight Storage Structure**

**File**: `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775476413559-8orraq/unsloth/kernels/utils.py` (lines 190-257)

The LoRA weights are stored as dictionaries indexed by adapter name:

```python
def get_lora_parameters(proj):
    """Return a 5-tuple of (weight, weight quant_state, lora A, lora B, and lora scale)."""
    
    adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapters", None)
    if adapter is None: 
        adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapter", ("default"))
    adapter = adapter[0]
    
    # LoRA A and B matrices stored as Linear layers
    lora_A_linear = proj.lora_A[adapter]      # Dictionary lookup by adapter name
    lora_B_linear = proj.lora_B[adapter]      # Dictionary lookup by adapter name
    
    A = lora_A_linear.weight    # Extract weight tensor from Linear layer
    B = lora_B_linear.weight    # Extract weight tensor from Linear layer
    
    return (
        W,                              # Base layer weight
        getattr(W, "quant_state", None),# Quantization state for 4-bit
        A,                              # LoRA A matrix
        B,                              # LoRA B matrix
        proj.scaling[adapter],          # LoRA scaling factor (lora_alpha / rank)
    )
```

**Key attributes accessed**:
- `proj.lora_A[adapter]` - Dictionary of Linear modules for projection A (shape: [rank, in_features])
- `proj.lora_B[adapter]` - Dictionary of Linear modules for projection B (shape: [out_features, rank])
- `proj.scaling[adapter]` - Scaling factor (typically lora_alpha value)
- `proj.active_adapters` or `proj.active_adapter` - Currently active adapter name (default is "default")

---

### **2. LoRA Forward Pass Computation**

**File**: `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775476413559-8orraq/unsloth/kernels/utils.py` (lines 734-757)

The LoRA computation uses the rank decomposition:

```python
def matmul_lora(X, W, W_quant, A, B, s, out = None):
    """Compute: out = X @ W + (X @ A) @ B * s
    where s is the scaling factor (lora_alpha / rank)
    """
    dtype = X.dtype
    W = fast_dequantize(W.t(), W_quant, use_global_buffer = True)
    
    # Base computation
    out = torch_matmul(X, W, out = out)
    
    # LoRA addition: output += (X @ A.T) @ B.T * scaling
    if A is not None:
        A, B = A.t(), B.t()
        XA = torch_matmul(X, A.to(dtype))
        out.addmm_(XA, B.to(dtype), alpha = s)  # in-place: out += (X @ A) @ B * s
    
    return out.view(batch, seq_len, -1) if reshape else out
```

---

### **3. LoRA Configuration Parameters**

**File**: `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775476413559-8orraq/unsloth/models/llama.py` (lines 2598-2617)

LoRA configuration is created via PEFT's LoraConfig:

```python
arguments = dict(
    r                   = r,                    # Rank (e.g., 16, 32, 64)
    lora_alpha          = lora_alpha,           # LoRA alpha (typically = rank)
    target_modules      = final_modules,        # Which modules to apply LoRA
    lora_dropout        = lora_dropout,         # Dropout for LoRA (usually 0.0)
    bias                = bias,                 # "none", "all", or "lora_only"
    task_type           = TaskType.CAUSAL_LM,  # Task type
    layers_to_transform = layers_to_transform,  # Specific layers (None = all)
    init_lora_weights   = init_lora_weights,   # Weight initialization
    loftq_config        = loftq_config,        # LoftQ initialization config
    use_rslora          = use_rslora,          # Rank-Stabilized LoRA
    modules_to_save     = modules_to_save,     # Modules to fully train
)

lora_config = LoraConfig(**arguments)
model = _get_peft_model(model, lora_config)
```

**Configuration parameters**:
- `r` - LoRA rank (controls A/B matrix dimensions)
- `lora_alpha` - Scaling parameter (used to compute `scaling = lora_alpha / rank`)
- `target_modules` - List of module names to apply LoRA (e.g., `["q_proj", "k_proj", "v_proj", "o_proj"]`)
- `modules_to_save` - Optional modules that are fully trained instead of LoRA

**Default target_modules** (from llama.py, line 2269-2270):
```python
["q_proj", "k_proj", "v_proj", "o_proj", "gate_proj", "up_proj", "down_proj"]
```

---

### **4. Configuration Storage and Retrieval**

**File**: `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775476413559-8orraq/unsloth/models/llama.py` (lines 2334-2349)

When a model already has LoRA adapters, configuration is accessed from:

```python
peft_config = model.peft_config["default"].to_dict()

check_parameters = [
    "r",                    # Rank
    "lora_alpha",          # Alpha value
    "lora_dropout",        # Dropout
    "bias",                # Bias configuration
    "layers_to_transform", # Specific layers
    "layers_pattern",      # Layer pattern matching
    "use_rslora",          # RS-LoRA flag
    "init_lora_weights",   # Weight initialization method
]

# Access target_modules
old_target_modules = list(peft_config["target_modules"])
modules_to_save = peft_config["modules_to_save"]
```

These configurations are stored in the **`adapter_config.json`** file when saved.

---

### **5. State Dict Storage and Merging**

**File**: `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775476413559-8orraq/unsloth/save.py` (lines 159-186)

When merging LoRA weights into the base model:

```python
def _merge_lora(layer, name):
    """Merge LoRA adapters into base weights"""
    
    if isinstance(layer, (Bnb_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear)):
        # Extract LoRA parameters
        W, quant_state, A, B, s, bias = get_lora_parameters_bias(layer)
        
        if quant_state is not None:
            # Dequantize 4-bit weights
            dtype = quant_state.dtype if type(quant_state) is not list else quant_state[2]
            W = fast_dequantize(W, quant_state)
        else:
            dtype = W.dtype
        
        W = W.to(torch.float32).t()
        
        if A is not None:
            # Merge: W = W + A.T @ (s * B.T)
            W.addmm_(A.t().to(torch.float32), B.t().to(torch.float32), alpha = s)
        
        W = W.t().to(dtype)
    else:
        W = layer.weight
    
    return W, bias
```

The merged state_dict is built layer-by-layer:
```python
state_dict = OrderedDict()
state_dict["model.embed_tokens.weight"] = ...

for j, layer in enumerate(internal_model.model.layers):
    for item in LLAMA_WEIGHTS:  # q_proj, k_proj, v_proj, o_proj, gate_proj, up_proj, down_proj
        proj = eval(f"layer.{item}")
        name = f"model.layers.{j}.{item}.weight"
        W, bias = _merge_lora(proj, name)
        state_dict[name] = W
```

---

### **6. Metadata Storage (Adapter Config JSON)**

**Files**: `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775476413559-8orraq/unsloth/save.py` (lines 1566-1580)

When saving adapters:
```python
# Save custom config.json for LoRA
with open("_temporary_unsloth_config.json", "w", encoding = "utf-8") as file:
    json.dump({"model_type" : model.config.model_type}, file, indent = 4)
```

The PEFT library automatically saves **`adapter_config.json`** containing:
```json
{
    "base_model_name_or_path": "...",
    "r": 16,
    "lora_alpha": 16,
    "target_modules": ["q_proj", "k_proj", "v_proj", "o_proj", "gate_proj", "up_proj", "down_proj"],
    "lora_dropout": 0.0,
    "bias": "none",
    "task_type": "CAUSAL_LM",
    "modules_to_save": null,
    "init_lora_weights": true,
    "use_rslora": false
}
```

The actual adapter weights are stored in **`adapter_model.safetensors`** or **`adapter_model.bin`** with keys like:
- `lora_A.default.weight` - Rank x in_features
- `lora_B.default.weight` - out_features x rank

---

### **7. Model Loading with LoRA Adapters**

**File**: `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775476413559-8orraq/unsloth/models/loader.py` (lines 224-235, 260-272)

When loading a model with LoRA:

```python
# Detect if it's a PEFT model
try:
    peft_config = PeftConfig.from_pretrained(
        model_name,
        token = token,
        revision = revision,
        trust_remote_code = trust_remote_code,
    )
    is_peft = True
except:
    is_peft = False

# Check for conflicting configs
exist_adapter_config = os.path.exists(os.path.join(model_name, "adapter_config.json"))
exist_config = os.path.exists(os.path.join(model_name, "config.json"))
both_exist = exist_adapter_config and exist_config  # Should not happen

# If PEFT, get base model
if is_peft:
    model_name = peft_config.base_model_name_or_path
```

---

### **8. Test Files Demonstrating LoRA Usage**

**File**: `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775476413559-8orraq/tests/saving/test_unsloth_save.py` (lines 45-90)

```python
# Load model with LoRA
model, tokenizer = FastModel.from_pretrained(
    model_name,
    max_seq_length=128,
    load_in_4bit=True,
)

# Apply LoRA configuration
model = FastModel.get_peft_model(
    model,
    r=16,                                                    # Rank
    target_modules=["q_proj", "k_proj", "v_proj", "o_proj"], # Target modules
    lora_alpha=16,                                           # Alpha
    use_gradient_checkpointing="unsloth",
)
```

---

### **9. Scaling Factor Computation**

The actual scaling used in forward pass is:
```
scaling[adapter] = lora_alpha / rank
```

This is automatically computed by PEFT and stored in `proj.scaling[adapter]`. In the forward pass:
```python
output += (X @ A.T) @ B.T * scaling  # where scaling ≈ lora_alpha/r
```

---

### **10. Key Files Summary**

| File Path | Purpose |
|-----------|---------|
| `/unsloth/kernels/utils.py` | `get_lora_parameters()`, weight extraction and access |
| `/unsloth/kernels/fast_lora.py` | Forward/backward LoRA computation, `matmul_lora()` |
| `/unsloth/models/llama.py` | `get_peft_model()` config creation, parameter handling |
| `/unsloth/models/loader.py` | Loading models with `adapter_config.json`, PEFT detection |
| `/unsloth/save.py` | `_merge_lora()` merging adapters into state_dict |
| `/unsloth/kernels/__init__.py` | Exports `get_lora_parameters`, `matmul_lora` |

---

### **Summary**

LoRA adapter weights in unsloth are:
1. **Stored** as dictionary of Linear modules indexed by adapter name (`lora_A[adapter]`, `lora_B[adapter]`)
2. **Configured** via PEFT's LoraConfig with rank, alpha, target_modules, etc.
3. **Accessed** via `get_lora_parameters()` which extracts A/B weight tensors and scaling factor
4. **Computed** using `matmul_lora()`: `output = X @ W + (X @ A.T) @ B.T * (alpha/rank)`
5. **Saved** as `adapter_config.json` + `adapter_model.safetensors` files
6. **Merged** using `_merge_lora()` by computing `W' = W + A.T @ B.T * scaling`

### **1. LoRA Weight Storage Structure**

**File**: `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775476413559-8orraq/unsloth/kernels/utils.py` (lines 190-257)

The LoRA weights are stored as dictionaries indexed by adapter name:

```python
def get_lora_parameters(proj):
    """Return a 5-tuple of (weight, weight quant_state, lora A, lora B, and lora scale)."""
    
    adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapters", None)
    if adapter is None: 
        adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapter", ("default"))
    adapter = adapter[0]
    
    # LoRA A and B matrices stored as Linear layers
    lora_A_linear = proj.lora_A[adapter]      # Dictionary lookup by adapter name
    lora_B_linear = proj.lora_B[adapter]      # Dictionary lookup by adapter name
    
    A = lora_A_linear.weight    # Extract weight tensor from Linear layer
    B = lora_B_linear.weight    # Extract weight tensor from Linear layer
    
    return (
        W,                              # Base layer weight
        getattr(W, "quant_state", None),# Quantization state for 4-bit
        A,                              # LoRA A matrix
        B,                              # LoRA B matrix
        proj.scaling[adapter],          # LoRA scaling factor (lora_alpha / rank)
    )
```

**Key attributes accessed**:
- `proj.lora_A[adapter]` - Dictionary of Linear modules for projection A (shape: [rank, in_features])
- `proj.lora_B[adapter]` - Dictionary of Linear modules for projection B (shape: [out_features, rank])
- `proj.scaling[adapter]` - Scaling factor (typically lora_alpha value)
- `proj.active_adapters` or `proj.active_adapter` - Currently active adapter name (default is "default")

### **2. LoRA Forward Pass Computation**

**File**: `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775476413559-8orraq/unsloth/kernels/utils.py` (lines 734-757)

The LoRA computation uses the rank decomposition:

```python
def matmul_lora(X, W, W_quant, A, B, s, out = None):
    """Compute: out = X @ W + (X @ A) @ B * s
    where s is the scaling factor (lora_alpha / rank)
    """
    dtype = X.dtype
    W = fast_dequantize(W.t(), W_quant, use_global_buffer = True)
    
    # Base computation
    out = torch_matmul(X, W, out = out)
    
    # LoRA addition: output += (X @ A.T) @ B.T * scaling
    if A is not None:
        A, B = A.t(), B.t()
        XA = torch_matmul(X, A.to(dtype))
        out.addmm_(XA, B.to(dtype), alpha = s)  # in-place: out += (X @ A) @ B * s
    
    return out.view(batch, seq_len, -1) if reshape else out
```

### **3. LoRA Configuration Parameters**

**File**: `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775476413559-8orraq/unsloth/models/llama.py` (lines 2598-2617)

LoRA configuration is created via PEFT's LoraConfig:

```python
arguments = dict(
    r                   = r,                    # Rank (e.g., 16, 32, 64)
    lora_alpha          = lora_alpha,           # LoRA alpha (typically = rank)
    target_modules      = final_modules,        # Which modules to apply LoRA
    lora_dropout        = lora_dropout,         # Dropout for LoRA (usually 0.0)
    bias                = bias,                 # "none", "all", or "lora_only"
    task_type           = TaskType.CAUSAL_LM,  # Task type
    layers_to_transform = layers_to_transform,  # Specific layers (None = all)
    init_lora_weights   = init_lora_weights,   # Weight initialization
    loftq_config        = loftq_config,        # LoftQ initialization config
    use_rslora          = use_rslora,          # Rank-Stabilized LoRA
    modules_to_save     = modules_to_save,     # Modules to fully train
)

lora_config = LoraConfig(**arguments)
model = _get_peft_model(model, lora_config)
```

**Configuration parameters**:
- `r` - LoRA rank (controls A/B matrix dimensions)
- `lora_alpha` - Scaling parameter (used to compute `scaling = lora_alpha / rank`)
- `target_modules` - List of module names to apply LoRA (e.g., `["q_proj", "k_proj", "v_proj", "o_proj"]`)
- `modules_to_save` - Optional modules that are fully trained instead of LoRA

**Default target_modules** (from llama.py, line 2269-2270):
```python
["q_proj", "k_proj", "v_proj", "o_proj", "gate_proj", "up_proj", "down_proj"]
```

### **5. State Dict Storage and Merging**

**File**: `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775476413559-8orraq/unsloth/save.py` (lines 159-186)

When merging LoRA weights into the base model:

```python
def _merge_lora(layer, name):
    """Merge LoRA adapters into base weights"""
    
    if isinstance(layer, (Bnb_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear)):
        # Extract LoRA parameters
        W, quant_state, A, B, s, bias = get_lora_parameters_bias(layer)
        
        if quant_state is not None:
            # Dequantize 4-bit weights
            dtype = quant_state.dtype if type(quant_state) is not list else quant_state[2]
            W = fast_dequantize(W, quant_state)
        else:
            dtype = W.dtype
        
        W = W.to(torch.float32).t()
        
        if A is not None:
            # Merge: W = W + A.T @ (s * B.T)
            W.addmm_(A.t().to(torch.float32), B.t().to(torch.float32), alpha = s)
        
        W = W.t().to(dtype)
    else:
        W = layer.weight
    
    return W, bias
```

The merged state_dict is built layer-by-layer:
```python
state_dict = OrderedDict()
state_dict["model.embed_tokens.weight"] = ...

for j, layer in enumerate(internal_model.model.layers):
    for item in LLAMA_WEIGHTS:  # q_proj, k_proj, v_proj, o_proj, gate_proj, up_proj, down_proj
        proj = eval(f"layer.{item}")
        name = f"model.layers.{j}.{item}.weight"
        W, bias = _merge_lora(proj, name)
        state_dict[name] = W
```

```json
    "use_rslora": false
}
```

The actual adapter weights are stored in **`adapter_model.safetensors`** or **`adapter_model.bin`** with keys like:

- `lora_B.default.weight` - out_features x rank

### **9. Scaling Factor Computation**

The actual scaling used in forward pass is:
```
scaling[adapter] = lora_alpha / rank
```

This is automatically computed by PEFT and stored in `proj.scaling[adapter]`. In the forward pass:
```python
output += (X @ A.T) @ B.T * scaling  # where scaling ≈ lora_alpha/r
```

### **Summary**

LoRA adapter weights in unsloth are:
1. **Stored** as dictionary of Linear modules indexed by adapter name (`lora_A[adapter]`, `lora_B[adapter]`)
2. **Configured** via PEFT's LoraConfig with rank, alpha, target_modules, etc.
3. **Accessed** via `get_lora_parameters()` which extracts A/B weight tensors and scaling factor
4. **Computed** using `matmul_lora()`: `output = X @ W + (X @ A.T) @ B.T * (alpha/rank)`
5. **Saved** as `adapter_config.json` + `adapter_model.safetensors` files
6. **Merged** using `_merge_lora()` by computing `W' = W + A.T @ B.T * scaling`

# Copyright 2023-present Daniel Han-Chen & the Unsloth team. All rights reserved.
#
# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.

# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
# limitations under the License.

import warnings, importlib, sys
from packaging.version import Version
import os, re, subprocess, inspect, functools
import numpy as np

# Fix some issues before importing other packages
from .import_fixes import fix_message_factory_issue
fix_message_factory_issue(); del fix_message_factory_issue;

# Check if modules that need patching are already imported
critical_modules = ['trl', 'transformers', 'peft']
already_imported = [mod for mod in critical_modules if mod in sys.modules]

# This check is critical because Unsloth optimizes these libraries by modifying
# their code at import time. If they're imported first, the original (slower,
# more memory-intensive) implementations will be used instead of Unsloth's
# optimized versions, potentially causing OOM errors or slower training.

if already_imported:
    # stacklevel=2 makes warning point to user's import line rather than this library code,
    # showing them exactly where to fix the import order in their script
    warnings.warn(
        f"WARNING: Unsloth should be imported before {', '.join(already_imported)} "
        f"to ensure all optimizations are applied. Your code may run slower or encounter "
        f"memory issues without these optimizations.\n\n"
        f"Please restructure your imports with 'import unsloth' at the top of your file.",
        stacklevel = 2,
    )
pass

# Unsloth currently does not work on multi GPU setups - sadly we are a 2 brother team so
# enabling it will require much more work, so we have to prioritize. Please understand!
# We do have a beta version, which you can contact us about!
# Thank you for your understanding and we appreciate it immensely!

# Fixes https://github.com/unslothai/unsloth/issues/1266
os.environ["PROTOCOL_BUFFERS_PYTHON_IMPLEMENTATION"] = "python"

# [TODO] Check why some GPUs don't work
#    "pinned_use_cuda_host_register:True,"\
#    "pinned_num_register_threads:8"

# Log Unsloth is being used
os.environ["UNSLOTH_IS_PRESENT"] = "1"

# Try importing PyTorch and check version
try:
    import torch
except ModuleNotFoundError:
    raise ImportError(
        "Unsloth: Pytorch is not installed. Go to https://pytorch.org/.\n"\
        "We have some installation instructions on our Github page."
    )
except Exception as exception:
    raise exception
pass

@functools.cache
def is_hip():
    return bool(getattr(getattr(torch, "version", None), "hip", None))
pass

@functools.cache
def get_device_type():
    if hasattr(torch, "cuda") and torch.cuda.is_available():
        if is_hip():
            return "hip"
        return "cuda"
    elif hasattr(torch, "xpu") and torch.xpu.is_available():
        return "xpu"
    raise NotImplementedError("Unsloth currently only works on NVIDIA GPUs and Intel GPUs.")
pass
DEVICE_TYPE : str = get_device_type()

@functools.cache
def get_device_count():
    if DEVICE_TYPE in ("cuda", "hip"):
        return torch.cuda.device_count()
    elif DEVICE_TYPE == "xpu":
        return torch.xpu.device_count()
    else:
        return 1
pass

DEVICE_COUNT : int = get_device_count()

# Reduce VRAM usage by reducing fragmentation
# And optimize pinning of memory
# TODO(billishyahao): need to add hip related optimization...
if (DEVICE_TYPE in ("cuda", "hip")) and (os.environ.get("UNSLOTH_VLLM_STANDBY", "0")=="0"):
    os.environ["PYTORCH_CUDA_ALLOC_CONF"] = \
        "expandable_segments:True,"\
        "roundup_power2_divisions:[32:256,64:128,256:64,>:32]"
elif (DEVICE_TYPE in ("cuda", "hip")) and (os.environ.get("UNSLOTH_VLLM_STANDBY", "0")=="1") and \
    ("expandable_segments:True" in os.environ.get("PYTORCH_CUDA_ALLOC_CONF", "")):
    warnings.warn(
        "Unsloth: `UNSLOTH_VLLM_STANDBY` is on, but requires `expandable_segments` to be off.\n"\
        "We will remove `expandable_segments`.",
        stacklevel = 2,
    )
    os.environ["PYTORCH_CUDA_ALLOC_CONF"] = re.sub(
        r"expandable\_segments\:True\,?",
        "",
        os.environ["PYTORCH_CUDA_ALLOC_CONF"],
    )
pass
# We support Pytorch 2
# Fixes https://github.com/unslothai/unsloth/issues/38
torch_version = str(re.match(r"[0-9\.]{3,}", str(torch.__version__)).group(0)).split(".")
major_torch, minor_torch = torch_version[0], torch_version[1]
major_torch, minor_torch = int(major_torch), int(minor_torch)
if (major_torch < 2):
    raise ImportError("Unsloth only supports Pytorch 2 for now. Please update your Pytorch to 2.1.\n"\
                      "We have some installation instructions on our Github page.")
elif (major_torch == 2) and (minor_torch < 2):
    # Disable expandable_segments
    del os.environ["PYTORCH_CUDA_ALLOC_CONF"]
pass

# CCE fails on Torch 2.8 and above
# OutOfResources: out of resource: shared memory, Required: 98304, Hardware limit: 65536. Reducing block sizes or `num_stages`
if (major_torch >= 2 and minor_torch >= 8) or (major_torch > 2):
    os.environ["UNSLOTH_ENABLE_CCE"] = "0"
pass

# Fix other issues
import importlib.util
from pathlib import Path
from importlib.metadata import version as importlib_version
from .import_fixes import fix_xformers_performance_issue
fix_xformers_performance_issue(); del fix_xformers_performance_issue;
from .import_fixes import fix_vllm_aimv2_issue
fix_vllm_aimv2_issue(); del fix_vllm_aimv2_issue;
from .import_fixes import ignore_logger_messages
ignore_logger_messages(); del ignore_logger_messages;

# Torch 2.4 has including_emulation
if DEVICE_TYPE == "cuda":
    major_version, minor_version = torch.cuda.get_device_capability()
    SUPPORTS_BFLOAT16 = (major_version >= 8)

    old_is_bf16_supported = torch.cuda.is_bf16_supported
    if "including_emulation" in str(inspect.signature(old_is_bf16_supported)):
        def is_bf16_supported(including_emulation = False):
            return old_is_bf16_supported(including_emulation)
        torch.cuda.is_bf16_supported = is_bf16_supported
    else:
        def is_bf16_supported(): return SUPPORTS_BFLOAT16
        torch.cuda.is_bf16_supported = is_bf16_supported
    pass
elif DEVICE_TYPE == "hip":
    SUPPORTS_BFLOAT16 = torch.cuda.is_bf16_supported()
elif DEVICE_TYPE == "xpu":
    # torch.xpu.is_bf16_supported() does not have including_emulation
    # set SUPPORTS_BFLOAT16 as torch.xpu.is_bf16_supported()
    SUPPORTS_BFLOAT16 = torch.xpu.is_bf16_supported()
pass

# For Gradio HF Spaces?
# if "SPACE_AUTHOR_NAME" not in os.environ and "SPACE_REPO_NAME" not in os.environ:
import triton
if DEVICE_TYPE == "cuda":
    libcuda_dirs = lambda: None
    if Version(triton.__version__) >= Version("3.0.0"):
        try: from triton.backends.nvidia.driver import libcuda_dirs
        except: pass
    else: from triton.common.build import libcuda_dirs

    # Try loading bitsandbytes and triton
    import bitsandbytes as bnb
    try:
        cdequantize_blockwise_fp32 = bnb.functional.lib.cdequantize_blockwise_fp32
        libcuda_dirs()
    except:
        warnings.warn(
            "Unsloth: Running `ldconfig /usr/lib64-nvidia` to link CUDA."\
        )

        if os.path.exists("/usr/lib64-nvidia"):
            os.system("ldconfig /usr/lib64-nvidia")
        elif os.path.exists("/usr/local"):
            # Sometimes bitsandbytes cannot be linked properly in Runpod for example
            possible_cudas = subprocess.check_output(["ls", "-al", "/usr/local"]).decode("utf-8").split("\n")
            find_cuda = re.compile(r"[\s](cuda\-[\d\.]{2,})$")
            possible_cudas = [find_cuda.search(x) for x in possible_cudas]
            possible_cudas = [x.group(1) for x in possible_cudas if x is not None]

            # Try linking cuda folder, or everything in local
            if len(possible_cudas) == 0:
                os.system("ldconfig /usr/local/")
            else:
                find_number = re.compile(r"([\d\.]{2,})")
                latest_cuda = np.argsort([float(find_number.search(x).group(1)) for x in possible_cudas])[::-1][0]
                latest_cuda = possible_cudas[latest_cuda]
                os.system(f"ldconfig /usr/local/{latest_cuda}")
        pass

        importlib.reload(bnb)
        importlib.reload(triton)
        try:
            libcuda_dirs = lambda: None
            if Version(triton.__version__) >= Version("3.0.0"):
                try: from triton.backends.nvidia.driver import libcuda_dirs
                except: pass
            else: from triton.common.build import libcuda_dirs
            cdequantize_blockwise_fp32 = bnb.functional.lib.cdequantize_blockwise_fp32
            libcuda_dirs()
        except:
            warnings.warn(
                "Unsloth: CUDA is not linked properly.\n"\
                "Try running `python -m bitsandbytes` then `python -m xformers.info`\n"\
                "We tried running `ldconfig /usr/lib64-nvidia` ourselves, but it didn't work.\n"\
                "You need to run in your terminal `sudo ldconfig /usr/lib64-nvidia` yourself, then import Unsloth.\n"\
                "Also try `sudo ldconfig /usr/local/cuda-xx.x` - find the latest cuda version.\n"\
                "Unsloth will still run for now, but maybe it might crash - let's hope it works!"
            )
    pass
elif DEVICE_TYPE == "hip":
    # NO-OP for rocm device
    pass
elif DEVICE_TYPE == "xpu":
    # currently intel xpu will not support bnb, will add support in the future
    # TODO: check triton for intel installed properly.
    pass

# Check for unsloth_zoo
try:
    unsloth_zoo_version = importlib_version("unsloth_zoo")
    if Version(unsloth_zoo_version) < Version("2025.9.6"):
        print(
            "Unsloth: Please update Unsloth and Unsloth-Zoo to the latest version!\n"\
            "Do this via `pip install --upgrade --force-reinstall --no-cache-dir --no-deps unsloth unsloth_zoo`"
        )
        # if os.environ.get("UNSLOTH_DISABLE_AUTO_UPDATES", "0") == "0":
        #     try:
        #         os.system("pip install --upgrade --no-cache-dir --no-deps unsloth_zoo")
        #     except:
        #         try:
        #             os.system("pip install --upgrade --no-cache-dir --no-deps --user unsloth_zoo")
        #         except:
        #             raise ImportError("Unsloth: Please update unsloth_zoo via `pip install --upgrade --no-cache-dir --no-deps unsloth_zoo`")
    import unsloth_zoo
except:
    raise ImportError("Unsloth: Please install unsloth_zoo via `pip install unsloth_zoo`")
pass

from .models import *
from .models import __version__
from .save import *
from .chat_templates import *
from .tokenizer_utils import *
from .trainer import *

# Patch TRL trainers for backwards compatibility
_patch_trl_trainer()

    original_model = model

        if original_model.push_to_hub.__name__ != "unsloth_push_to_hub":

            if hasattr(original_model, "add_model_tags"):

        if hasattr(original_model, "model"): original_model = original_model.model
        else: break
    pass

    # Add saving methods to top level model
    if not vision:
        if hasattr(model, "config"):
            # Counteract tokenizers
            model.push_to_hub_merged     = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_push_to_hub_merged,            model)
            model.save_pretrained_merged = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged,        model)
            model.push_to_hub_gguf       = types.MethodType(unsloth_push_to_hub_gguf,                      model)
            model.save_pretrained_gguf   = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_gguf,                  model)
            model.save_pretrained_torchao   = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao,            model)
            model.push_to_hub_ggml       = types.MethodType(unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_push_to_hub,  model)
            model.save_pretrained_ggml   = types.MethodType(unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_save_locally, model)
        pass
    else:
        # Vision only 1 option
        model.push_to_hub_merged     = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_push_to_hub_merged,     model)
        model.save_pretrained_merged = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged, model)
        model.push_to_hub_gguf       = types.MethodType(save_to_gguf_generic,                   model)
        model.save_pretrained_gguf   = types.MethodType(save_to_gguf_generic,                   model)
        model.save_pretrained_torchao = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao,       model)
    pass
    return model

# Go through every cached repo, and delete the one that matches the model we want to save.
    # Can save 4GB of disk space - useful for Kaggle systems.
    for cached_repo in cached_repos:
        if cached_repo.repo_id == model.config._name_or_path:
            remove_cache_commit = list(cached_repo.revisions)[0].commit_hash
            delete_strategy = scan_cache_dir().delete_revisions(remove_cache_commit,)

            logger.warning_once(
                "Unsloth: Will remove a cached repo with size " + \
                delete_strategy.expected_freed_size_str,
            )

            delete_strategy.execute()
        pass
    pass
pass


def _merge_lora(layer, name):

    bias = getattr(layer, "bias", None)
    if isinstance(layer, (Bnb_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear)):
        # Is LoRA so we need to merge!
        W, quant_state, A, B, s, bias = get_lora_parameters_bias(layer)
        if quant_state is not None:
            dtype = quant_state.dtype if type(quant_state) is not list else quant_state[2]
            W = fast_dequantize(W, quant_state)
        else:
            dtype = W.dtype
        W = W.to(torch.float32).t()
        # W = W.t()

        if A is not None:
            # sAB = (A.t().to(torch.float32) @ (s * B.t().to(torch.float32)))
            # W += sAB
            W.addmm_(A.t().to(torch.float32), B.t().to(torch.float32), alpha = s)
            # W.addmm_(A.t().to(W.dtype), B.t().to(W.dtype), alpha = s)
            # if not torch.isfinite(W).all():
            maximum_element = torch.max(W.min().abs(), W.max())
            if not torch.isfinite(maximum_element).item():
                raise ValueError(f"Unsloth: Merge failed.\n{name} has some elements = infinity.")
        pass
        W = W.t().to(dtype)
    else:
        W = layer.weight
    return W, bias
pass


def fast_save_pickle(shard, name):
    # Use this if # CPUs is <= 2
    print(f"Unsloth: Saving {name}...")
    torch.save(
        shard,
        name,
        # HIGHEST_PROTOCOL seems to not work with Pytorch!
        # pickle_module   = pickle,
        # pickle_protocol = pickle.HIGHEST_PROTOCOL,
    )
    return
pass


@torch.inference_mode
def unsloth_save_model(
    model,
    tokenizer,
    save_directory       : Union[str, os.PathLike],
    save_method          : str = "lora", # ["lora", "merged_16bit", "merged_4bit"]
    push_to_hub          : bool = False,
    token                : Optional[Union[str, bool]] = None,
    is_main_process      : bool = True,
    state_dict           : Optional[dict] = None,
    save_function        : Callable = torch.save,
    max_shard_size       : Union[int, str] = "5GB",

def unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_save_locally(
    self,
    save_directory: str, # Added parameter for the folder name
    tokenizer,
    temporary_location: str = "_unsloth_temporary_saved_buffers",
    maximum_memory_usage: float = 0.85,
):
    if not os.path.exists("llama.cpp"):
        if IS_KAGGLE_ENVIRONMENT:
            python_install = install_python_non_blocking(["protobuf"])
            python_install.wait()
            install_llama_cpp_blocking(use_cuda=False)
            makefile = None
        else:
            git_clone = install_llama_cpp_clone_non_blocking()
            python_install = install_python_non_blocking(["protobuf"])
            git_clone.wait()
            makefile = install_llama_cpp_make_non_blocking()
            python_install.wait()
    else:
        makefile = None

    for _ in range(3):
        gc.collect()

    # Use the provided save_directory for local saving
    save_lora_to_custom_dir(self, tokenizer, save_directory)

    model_type = self.config.model_type
    output_file = os.path.join(save_directory, "ggml-adapter-model.bin")

    print(f"Unsloth: Converting auto-saved LoRA adapters at {save_directory} to GGML format.")
    print(f"The output file will be {output_file}")

    command = f"python3 llama.cpp/convert-lora-to-ggml.py {save_directory} {output_file} llama"

    try:
        with subprocess.Popen(command, shell=True, stdout=subprocess.PIPE, stderr=subprocess.PIPE, bufsize=1, universal_newlines=True) as sp:
            for line in sp.stdout:
                print(line, end="", flush=True)
            for line in sp.stderr:
                print(line, end="", flush=True)
            sp.wait()
            if sp.returncode != 0:
                raise subprocess.CalledProcessError(sp.returncode, command)
    except subprocess.CalledProcessError as e:
        print(f"Error: Conversion failed with return code {e.returncode}")
        return
    print("Unsloth: Done.")
    print(f"Unsloth: Conversion completed! Output file: {output_file}")
    print("\nThis GGML making function was made by Maheswar. Ping him @Maheswar on the Unsloth Discord or on HuggingFace (@mahiatlinux) if you like this!")
pass


from .models.loader_utils import get_model_name
from unsloth_zoo.saving_utils import (
    merge_and_overwrite_lora,
    prepare_saving,
)
from unsloth_zoo.llama_cpp import (
    install_llama_cpp,
    convert_to_gguf as _convert_to_gguf,
)

@torch.inference_mode
def save_to_gguf_generic(
    model,
    save_directory,
    tokenizer,
    quantization_method = None,
    quantization_type = "Q8_0",
    repo_id = None,
    token = None,
):
    if token is None and repo_id is not None: token = get_token()
    if repo_id is not None and token is None:
        raise RuntimeError("Unsloth: Please specify a token for uploading!")

    if not os.path.exists(os.path.join("llama.cpp", "unsloth_convert_hf_to_gguf.py")):
        install_llama_cpp(just_clone_repo = True)
    pass

    # Use old style quantization_method
    new_quantization_methods = []
    if quantization_method is not None:
        # Convert quantization_method to list
        if   isinstance(quantization_method, list):  pass
        elif isinstance(quantization_method, str):   quantization_method = [ quantization_method, ]
        elif isinstance(quantization_method, tuple): quantization_method = list(quantization_method)
        else:
            raise TypeError("Unsloth: quantization_method can only be a string or a list of strings")
        pass
        for i, quant_method in enumerate(quantization_method):
            quant_method = quant_method.lower()
            if   quant_method == "not_quantized":  quant_method = "f16"
            elif quant_method == "fast_quantized": quant_method = "q8_0"
            elif quant_method == "quantized":      quant_method = "q4_k_m"
            elif quant_method is None:             quant_method = "q8_0"
            new_quantization_methods.append(quant_method.lower())

    self,
    save_directory       : Union[str, os.PathLike],
    tokenizer            = None,
    save_method          : str = "merged_16bit", # ["lora", "merged_16bit", "merged_4bit"]
    push_to_hub          : bool = False,
    token                : Optional[Union[str, bool]] = None,
    is_main_process      : bool = True,
    state_dict           : Optional[dict] = None,
    save_function        : Callable = torch.save,
    max_shard_size       : Union[int, str] = "5GB",
    safe_serialization   : bool = True,
    variant              : Optional[str] = None,
    save_peft_format     : bool = True,
    tags                 : List[str] = None,
    temporary_location   : str = "_unsloth_temporary_saved_buffers",
    maximum_memory_usage : float = 0.75,
):
    """
        Same as .push_to_hub(...) except 4bit weights are auto
        converted to float16 with as few overhead as possible.

        Choose for `save_method` to be either:
        1. `16bit`: Merge LoRA into float16 weights. Useful for GGUF / llama.cpp.
        2.  `4bit`: Merge LoRA into int4 weights. Useful for DPO / HF inference.
        3.  `lora`: Save LoRA adapters with no merging. Useful for HF inference.
    """
    if tokenizer is None:
        logger.warning_once(
            "Unsloth: You're not saving a tokenizer as well?\n"\
            "You can do it separately via `tokenizer.save_pretrained(...)`"
        )
    pass

    arguments = dict(locals())
    arguments["model"] = self
    del arguments["self"]
    unsloth_generic_save(**arguments)
    for _ in range(3):
        gc.collect()
pass


def unsloth_generic_push_to_hub_merged(
    self,
    repo_id              : str,
    tokenizer            = None,
    save_method          : str = "merged_16bit", # ["lora", "merged_16bit", "merged_4bit"]
    use_temp_dir         : Optional[bool] = None,
    commit_message       : Optional[str] = "Trained with Unsloth",
    private              : Optional[bool] = None,
    token                : Union[bool, str, None] = None,
    max_shard_size       : Union[int, str, None] = "5GB",
    create_pr            : bool = False,
    safe_serialization   : bool = True,
    revision             : str = None,
    commit_description   : str = "Upload model trained with Unsloth 2x faster",
    tags                 : Optional[List[str]] = None,
    temporary_location   : str = "_unsloth_temporary_saved_buffers",
    maximum_memory_usage : float = 0.75,
):
    """
        Same as .push_to_hub(...) except 4bit weights are auto
        converted to float16 with as few overhead as possible.

        Choose for `save_method` to be either:
        1. `16bit`: Merge LoRA into float16 weights. Useful for GGUF / llama.cpp.
        2.  `4bit`: Merge LoRA into int4 weights. Useful for DPO / HF inference.
        3.  `lora`: Save LoRA adapters with no merging. Useful for HF inference.
    """
    if tokenizer is None:
        logger.warning_once(
            "Unsloth: You're not saving a tokenizer as well?\n"\
            "You can do it separately via `tokenizer.push_to_hub(...)`"
        )
    pass

    arguments = dict(locals())
    arguments["model"]          = self
    arguments["save_directory"] = repo_id

Now I have a thorough understanding of the codebase. Let me now design the implementation plan for adding an `export_to_onnx` method.

Here's what I need to do:

1. **Add a standalone `export_lora_to_onnx` function** in `unsloth/save.py`
   - This function will:
     - Extract LoRA A/B matrices from each LoRA-patched layer
     - Save them as ONNX models (one per layer/module)
     - Save metadata JSON with rank, alpha, target_modules
     - Accept opset_version parameter (default 17)

2. **Add the `save_pretrained_onnx` instance method** that wraps the standalone function
   
3. **Patch the model** in `patch_saving_functions` to add `model.save_pretrained_onnx`

4. **Update `__all__`** to export the function

Now I'm thinking through the design more carefully. The core idea is that we're exporting just the LoRA adapter weights—the A and B matrices—as ONNX tensors rather than the full model, which is a much lighter export format.

For each LoRA layer, I need to extract the A matrix (shape [rank, in_features]) and B matrix (shape [out_features, rank]), then decide whether to bundle everything into a single ONNX file with all adapters as initializers or create separate files per layer. A single consolidated ONNX file with named tensors seems cleaner and more practical than managing multiple files.

But I'm realizing I need to clarify what the export actually represents—are we just saving the weight matrices as constants, or should we encode the actual computation (input → lora_A @ input → lora_B @ result) as an ONNX graph? The user's request to "export LoRA adapter weights" suggests just saving the matrices themselves, though ONNX is typically used for computation graphs rather than raw tensor storage.

Looking at how ONNX Runtime handles LoRA adapters in practice, the standard approach is to save each adapter pair as a separate ONNX file containing the A and B matrices as computation nodes, or alternatively store all adapters as initializers in a single file for better interoperability.

For this use case, I think the cleanest solution is creating one ONNX file that holds all the LoRA weights as named initializers—essentially a weight-only model with no computation graph. The initializer names would follow the standard pattern like `base_model.model.layers.{i}.{module_name}.lora_A.weight` to maintain compatibility with existing tools.

Now I'm weighing the trade-offs between a single consolidated file versus separate files per adapter, considering what makes most sense for ONNX's structure and how these weights would actually be loaded and used downstream.

Writing the export function... Now I'm extracting the LoRA configuration metadata from the model and then iterating through its modules to collect the adapter weights from the linear layers that support LoRA. Building the ONNX graph...

Now I'm constructing the metadata dictionary with the LoRA configuration details—rank, alpha, target modules, and bias settings from the PEFT config—then saving it as JSON alongside the model file. I'm patching the model's save method and updating the exports, then reconsidering the ONNX structure—a weight-only model with just initializers is actually a valid and common pattern for serving, though I'm exploring if there's a cleaner semantic approach. Building the ONNX graph structure... I'll convert the weights to float32 for broader compatibility and include the shape information from the arrays when defining the ONNX outputs. Now I'm structuring the export function with proper error handling and imports, setting up the decorator and parameters for the ONNX export process.

Writing the export function... Extracting the LoRA weights and converting them to numpy arrays, then creating ONNX initializers and tensor value info objects for both the A and B matrices.

Now I'm finishing the tensor info for the B matrix, collecting all adapter layer names, and validating that at least some LoRA adapters were found. Then I'm constructing the ONNX graph with no operations—just the initializers exposed as outputs—and building the model proto with the appropriate IR version and opset imports before saving it to disk.

Saving the ONNX file and metadata JSON...

Now I'm planning out the exact changes needed to `unsloth/save.py`: I need to add `export_lora_to_onnx` to the `__all__` exports, insert the standalone function and the instance method in the right locations within the file, and then update the `patch_saving_functions` to wire everything together.

Looking at the existing pattern in the code, I can see how the non-vision and vision branches handle method binding differently. I need to add the ONNX method binding in both branches, following the same structure as the other save methods like `save_pretrained_merged` and `save_pretrained_gguf`. I need to add the ONNX export functionality to the save module, which means creating the export function and instance method, updating the exports list, and integrating it into the patching system so users can call it directly from the model object. The implementation is straightforward since the module already handles imports automatically through the init file.
